model_findings.description: The proxy accepts any --host value and passes it directly to uvicorn without an explicit remote-exposure opt-in or authentication. Although the default is 127.0.0.1, a user or wrapper can bind 0.0.0.0 and expose local agent CLIs and their credentials/configuration to the network; this conflicts with the documented localhost-only boundary and can let remote callers run costly or sensitive model requests.

Chat messages accept arbitrary-size strings and content lists with no request body, message count, or prompt length limit before flattening and forwarding to subprocesses. A large request can consume memory in FastAPI/Pydantic, produce a huge CLI argument/stdin payload, and amplify resource usage in backend agents.

Each /v1/chat/completions request synchronously launches a backend CLI, but the service has no concurrency limit, queue, rate limit, or admission control. Multiple clients or retries from OpenAI-compatible SDKs can spawn many long-running agent processes at once, exhausting CPU, memory, API quotas, or local CLI session limits even with the per-process timeout.

The request model allows extra OpenAI fields, but chat_completions ignores stream=true and still returns a normal JSON completion. OpenAI-compatible clients that request streaming will expect an SSE response and may hang, fail parsing, or incorrectly treat the proxy as streaming-capable; unsupported streaming should be rejected explicitly until implemented.

The gate only requires sentence and error, so an error-correction item with no answer or correction passes activity_schema even though ActivityParser later renders it as correctForm="" via i.get('answer') or i.get('correction', ''). That leaves a broken learner-facing activity accepted by the new gate; the schema needs a one-of requirement for answer/correction, with correction preferred as the canonical authoring field.

The item whitelist omits error_type, but current activity authoring examples/prompts include error_type as an optional error-correction item field. Any writer output following that contract will now fail as a forbidden field even though it is not one of the alias mistakes this gate is meant to catch, causing false rejection of otherwise valid generated activities.

If an error-correction activity has items present but not as a list, or contains non-mapping entries, the gate silently continues and can return passed with checked=0. Those shapes are not parseable by ActivityParser and should be schema violations; otherwise the new early gate can pass malformed writer output and defer the failure until later rendering/build steps.
